fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: District of Columbia Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to District of Columbia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity district of columbia, how to transfer audiologist license to district of columbia, audiologist compact district of columbia, district of columbia audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: DC Department of Health (DOH), Health Regulation and Licensing Administration
board.mailing_address: 899 North Capitol St. NE, Washington, DC 20002
board.physical_address: 899 North Capitol St. NE, Washington, DC 20002

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in District of Columbia.

steps.title: Meet the Education Requirements
steps.description: You must have graduated with a doctoral degree in audiology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Pass the Required Examination
steps.description: You must pass the Audiology Examination administered by Praxis.

steps.title: Submit an Application
steps.description: Complete the online application, pay the required fee, and submit all supporting documentation to the Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a District of Columbia audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in District of Columbia
hero.dek: District of Columbia Audiologist licenses require graduation from an approved program, passing the Audiology Examination administered by Praxis, and a completed application.
